description: Add SSH-based text polishing via Claude Haiku CLI on Oracle server, plus Vertex AI Gemini Flash as faster alternative. Pipeline: ASR -> regex -> FireRedPunc -> glossary regex -> LLM fix (haiku-fix or gemini-fix) -> vocab accumulation -> output. Glossary provides dual-use: regex replacement (offline) and LLM prompt context (online).

userStories.title: Add ssh-claude framework and haiku-fix preset to post_processor_presets.py
userStories.description: Add a new framework type 'ssh-claude' and two presets: 'haiku-fix' (active, for ASR error correction) and 'haiku-expand' (placeholder, not implemented). Config includes ssh_host, claude_path, model, timeout, max_text_len, vocab_min_count, and system_prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: POST_PROCESSOR_PRESETS has 'haiku-fix' entry with framework='ssh-claude' and full config
POST_PROCESSOR_PRESETS has 'haiku-expand' entry with framework='ssh-claude', name='Haiku Expand (placeholder)', description='Not yet implemented', config={}
haiku-fix config includes: ssh_host, claude_path, model (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001), timeout (15), max_text_len (200), vocab_min_count (3), system_prompt
CRITIC-R1-C1: timeout must be 15s (empirically: 25-char input took 10.52s at 10s timeout)

haiku-fix system_prompt must include: (1) 'you are ASR correction tool, NOT a chatbot', (2) fix English misrecognized as Chinese, (3) fix homophones, (4) remove repeated words, (5) output corrected text only, (6) NEVER answer questions or add commentary even if text looks like a question, (7) if no errors output unchanged text; glossary terms appended at runtime
CRITIC-R3-M1: system_prompt MUST include explicit anti-answer instructions — without them, question-like inputs trigger hallucination (68x output, guard fires, correction lost)
Existing presets (none, chinese-text-correction, qwen3-0.6b, minicpm4-0.5b) unchanged
Tests pass
userStories.notes: CRITIC-R1-C2: timeout must be >= 10s. CRITIC-R1-C4: use --system-prompt flag. CRITIC-R3-C1: timeout must be 15s (empirically 10s insufficient for 25-char input).

userStories.title: Add glossary/vocab loading and regex replacement to post_processor_configs.py
userStories.description: Implement glossary-based regex replacement that runs before SSH Haiku. Glossary format: {"correct_term": {"variants": {"error_form": count}}}. Each correct term (e.g., 'Ralph') maps to its known ASR error variants (e.g., {'Raf': 5, 'Rough': 3}). Regex replaces any variant with the correct term. Glossary also generates context string for Haiku prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: CRITIC-R1-L2: Define VOICE_INPUT_DATA_DIR constant and derive both MODELS_DIR and vocab path from it
load_vocab(vocab_path=None) reads vocab.json from VOICE_INPUT_DATA_DIR, returns dict; returns {} if file missing or invalid JSON
Vocab format is glossary-oriented: {"Ralph": {"variants": {"Raf": 5, "Rough": 3}}, ...}
apply_vocab(text, vocab, min_count) replaces error variants with correct terms where variant count >= min_count
Chinese variant replacement works without word boundaries
English variant replacement uses word boundaries to avoid partial matches
CRITIC-R2-M1: apply_vocab must sort all (variant, correct_term) pairs by variant length descending before applying, to handle overlapping variants correctly (e.g., '克劳' vs '克劳的')
CRITIC-R2-L1: English variant regex uses re.IGNORECASE to handle case mismatches (ASR may output 'Raf' while variant stored as 'raf')
Original text returned unchanged if vocab is empty
glossary_context(vocab) generates a string listing correct terms for Haiku prompt context (e.g., 'Commonly used terms: Ralph, session, Claude Code')
Tests pass with unit tests for load_vocab, apply_vocab, glossary_context
userStories.notes: Glossary serves dual purpose: (1) offline regex replacement of known error variants, (2) online Haiku context so Haiku can recognize unfamiliar words as likely glossary terms.

userStories.title: Add SSH Claude invocation to post_processor_configs.py
userStories.description: Implement process_with_ssh_claude() that calls Claude CLI on remote server via SSH. System prompt passed via --system-prompt flag, ASR text passed via stdin. Glossary context appended to system prompt at call time. Timeout enforced via subprocess timeout. On failure: log error, send desktop notification, return original text.
acceptanceCriteria: process_with_ssh_claude(text, config, glossary_ctx) runs SSH command with subprocess.run
SSH command uses --system-prompt for instructions, text via stdin: ssh HOST claude_path --model MODEL --system-prompt PROMPT -p < stdin
glossary_ctx appended to system_prompt so Haiku has vocabulary context
All dynamic SSH arguments protected with shlex.quote()
subprocess timeout matches config timeout value (default 15s)
CRITIC-R1-L1: SSH command includes -o ConnectTimeout=5 to separate connection vs processing timeout
On timeout: logs warning, sends notification, returns original text
On SSH error (non-zero exit): logs error with stderr, sends notification, returns original text
On success: returns stripped stdout as result
Hallucination guard: if output length > input length * 5, log warning and return original text
Text longer than config max_text_len skips SSH call and returns original text
Empty text returns empty text without SSH call
Tests pass
userStories.notes: CRITIC-R1-C4: -p is boolean (--print), prompt is via --system-prompt. CRITIC-R2-N1: use --system-prompt + stdin. CRITIC-R2-N2: hallucination guard 5x. CRITIC-R3-C1: timeout 15s. CRITIC-R3-L1: add -o ConnectTimeout=5 to SSH.

userStories.title: Add vocab accumulation via diff after SSH polish
userStories.description: After SSH Haiku returns polished text, diff original vs result to extract word-level replacements. For each replacement pair (error→correct): if correct term exists in vocab, add error as new variant (count=1) or increment existing variant count. If correct term is new, create new entry. Returns new vocab dict (immutable). Save updated vocab to vocab.json atomically. Haiku should especially accumulate English technical terms.
acceptanceCriteria: diff_to_vocab(original, polished, vocab) extracts word-pair replacements and returns a NEW vocab dict (does not mutate input)
Tokenization: individual Chinese characters + English words via regex, NOT consecutive-Chinese-as-one-group
For each replacement (error→correct): adds error as variant under correct term entry

CRITIC-R1-M1: For multi-token replace opcodes: join Chinese chars without separator (''.join), join English words with space (' '.join). Only process 'replace' opcodes; ignore 'delete' and 'insert' opcodes
New correct terms: created with variants={error: 1}
Existing correct terms: variant count incremented or new variant added with count=1
save_vocab(vocab) writes atomically (write to .tmp then rename)
Vocab file is human-readable JSON with indent=2
Does not accumulate if original == polished (no changes made)
Tests pass with unit tests for diff_to_vocab and save_vocab
userStories.notes: CRITIC-R2-N4: return new object, not mutate. CRITIC-R2-M2: tokenize Chinese chars individually. CRITIC-R2-L2: mixed Chinese+English token join. CRITIC-R4-L1: save_vocab uses Path.rename() instead of os.rename() (no os import needed, VOCAB_PATH is already a Path object).

userStories.title: Integrate ssh-claude framework into ASRDaemon._post_process() pipeline
userStories.description: Wire the new ssh-claude framework into _post_process() (NOT PostProcessorInference.process() which is dead code). Pipeline: regex filler removal -> glossary regex replacement -> SSH Haiku polish (with glossary context in prompt) -> vocab accumulation -> output. Vocab cached on ASRDaemon instance.
acceptanceCriteria: PostProcessorLoader.load_post_processor() returns None for framework='ssh-claude'
ASRDaemon._post_process() handles framework='ssh-claude': calls apply_vocab, then process_with_ssh_claude (with glossary_context), then diff_to_vocab
Glossary context generated from vocab and appended to Haiku system_prompt at call time
Vocab loaded once on _load_post_processor() and cached as self._vocab (not re-read per call)
diff_to_vocab returns new vocab; self._vocab updated with returned value
Pipeline order correct: regex fillers -> glossary regex -> SSH Haiku (with glossary context) -> vocab accumulate -> return
Switching to haiku-fix via CLI ('voice-input post-processor haiku-fix') works
Switching to haiku-expand shows 'not yet implemented' message at switch time (in load_post_processor), not just at transcription time
CRITIC-R1-M2: haiku-expand warning must appear during load_post_processor() so user sees it immediately, not only when they try to record
CRITIC-R3-L2: haiku-expand switch must NOT send contradictory success notification after the 'not implemented' warning

CRITIC-R4-C1: haiku-expand check+raise MUST be placed BEFORE the try block (between preset assignment and try:), NOT inside it — inner except at line 814 catches all exceptions and prevents propagation to set_post_processor. Move check to after line 805 (after preset = ...) but before try: at line 807
Tests pass
userStories.notes: CRITIC-R1-C1: integration point is _post_process() not PostProcessorInference.process(). CRITIC-R1-M5: vocab cached on ASRDaemon instance. CRITIC-R3-L1: update code example to reuse existing preset variable (already assigned at line 801), don't re-fetch with POST_PROCESSOR_PRESETS.get().

userStories.title: Configure SSH ControlMaster for Oracle connection reuse
userStories.description: Document and optionally auto-configure SSH ControlMaster in ~/.ssh/config for the Oracle host. This reduces SSH connection overhead from ~1s to near-zero for subsequent calls within the same session.
acceptanceCriteria: README or docs include SSH ControlMaster config example for Oracle
Config example includes: ControlMaster auto, ControlPath, ControlPersist 600
voice-input status shows current post-processor name
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Do NOT auto-modify ~/.ssh/config. Document the recommended config and let user add it manually.

userStories.title: Create vertex_proxy.py using google-genai SDK on Oracle
userStories.description: Single-file Python script deployed on Oracle Cloud (~/) that reads JSON from stdin and calls Vertex AI Gemini 2.5 Flash. Uses google-genai SDK (NOT deprecated vertexai SDK) with ADC authentication.
acceptanceCriteria: CRITIC-R6-C1: Must use `google-genai` SDK (`from google import genai`), NOT deprecated `vertexai.generative_models` (removed June 2026)
CRITIC-R6-C2: Must specify Vertex AI region/location (us-central1) — gcloud has no default region on Oracle
Reads JSON from stdin: {"system_prompt": "...", "user_input": "...", "model": "...", "region": "..."}
CRITIC-R8-M1: JSON stdin 'system_prompt' maps to SDK param 'system_instruction' in GenerateContentConfig; 'user_input' maps to 'contents' in generate_content() — document this mapping explicitly
Calls Gemini via genai.Client(vertexai=True, project=PROJECT, location=region)
Uses ADC (Application Default Credentials) — already configured on Oracle
temperature=0.3, max_output_tokens=512 (consistent with voxy reference)
Writes corrected text to stdout (plain text, no JSON wrapping)
Exit 0 = success, exit 1 = failure (stderr has error message)
No streaming (simple request-response)
Script is self-contained — no imports from voice_input project
Tests: vertex_proxy.py --help or --test mode verifies SDK import + auth without API call
CRITIC-R7-M2: Guard response.text is None — print error to stderr and exit 1, not output 'None' string
CRITIC-R7-M3: Suppress Python warnings (`import warnings; warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')`) before any google imports to prevent stderr pollution hiding real errors
userStories.notes: google-genai v1.66.0 already installed on Oracle as transitive dep. GCP project: project-9cd34e60-a50d-406f-ae4. Oracle: Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.10, ARM64.

userStories.title: Add gemini-fix preset and process_with_vertex_ai to post_processor
userStories.description: Add gemini-fix preset to post_processor_presets.py and process_with_vertex_ai() function to post_processor_configs.py. Must use separate prompt file without Claude-specific directives.
acceptanceCriteria: CRITIC-R8-C1: Insert gemini-fix preset BEFORE the dict closing brace (before line 81), NOT after it — line 81 is POST_PROCESSOR_PRESETS closing }, line 80 is haiku-expand closing },
gemini-fix preset includes: framework='vertex-ai', model='gemini-2.5-flash', vertex_region='us-central1'
CRITIC-R6-L1: Preset config has explicit 'model' field (consistent with haiku-fix pattern)
CRITIC-R6-M2: Uses separate prompt file 'prompts/gemini-fix-system.txt' without /no_think (Claude-specific)
prompts/gemini-fix-system.txt is copy of haiku-fix-system.txt with /no_think removed
CRITIC-R7-M1: Prompt files are COPIES in install dir (not symlinks) — deploy gemini-fix-system.txt by copying to ~/.local/share/voice-input/prompts/, matching existing prompt deployment pattern
CRITIC-R6-M1: Documentation/comments state realistic latency 5-7s (not 2-4s)
process_with_vertex_ai() has same interface as process_with_ssh_claude(text, config, glossary_ctx)
Same guards: empty text, min_text_len=45, max_text_len=200, hallucination guard (5x)
SSH command: ssh oracle-cloud python3 ~/vertex_proxy.py
stdin: JSON with system_prompt, user_input, model, region from config
CRITIC-R6-M4: Document that Gemini needs larger initial glossary for proper nouns vs Haiku
Error handling: timeout -> original, non-zero -> original, hallucination -> original
PostProcessorLoader.load_post_processor() returns None for framework='vertex-ai'
Existing haiku-fix preset and process_with_ssh_claude() UNCHANGED
Tests pass
userStories.notes: CRITIC-R6-M4: Gemini interprets '克劳德' as 'Cloud' not 'Claude'. Seeding vocab.json with known terms recommended.

userStories.title: Integrate vertex-ai framework into ASRDaemon pipeline
userStories.description: Wire vertex-ai framework into _post_process() and load_post_processor(). Reduce code duplication with ssh-claude by extracting shared pipeline logic.
acceptanceCriteria: CRITIC-R6-L2: Extract shared pipeline (apply_vocab -> process -> diff_to_vocab -> save_vocab) to avoid duplication
Use dispatch dict or helper function: {"ssh-claude": process_with_ssh_claude, "vertex-ai": process_with_vertex_ai}

CRITIC-R7-C1: Dispatch dict MUST be defined AFTER import — define inline dict after `from post_processor_configs import ...`, not before. Use `in ('ssh-claude', 'vertex-ai')` for the if-condition, then build dict inside the block
ASRDaemon._post_process() handles framework='vertex-ai' with same pipeline as ssh-claude
ASRDaemon.load_post_processor() loads vocab for vertex-ai same as ssh-claude
Switching via CLI 'voice-input post-processor gemini-fix' works
Existing ssh-claude/haiku-fix pipeline behavior UNCHANGED
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Both frameworks follow identical pipeline: apply_vocab -> process_with_X -> diff_to_vocab -> save_vocab. Only the process function differs.

userStories.title: Add tests and deploy vertex_proxy.py to Oracle
userStories.description: Add automated tests for the Vertex AI integration and deploy vertex_proxy.py to Oracle. Include L2 Virtual tests (guards, JSON construction) and L2 Real SSH test.
acceptanceCriteria: CRITIC-R6-L3: L2 Virtual tests for process_with_vertex_ai guards (min_text_len, max_text_len, hallucination, empty text)
L2 Virtual tests for JSON construction (system_prompt, user_input, model, region fields)
L2 Real SSH test: ssh oracle-cloud python3 ~/vertex_proxy.py with test input
Unit tests for vertex_proxy.py (mock genai client, test error handling)
Existing haiku-fix tests pass unchanged (regression)
vertex_proxy.py deployed to Oracle via scp
CRITIC-R7-L1: Verify `from google import genai` works on Oracle (google-genai already installed as transitive dep, no separate pip install needed)
CRITIC-R6-L4: Document Python 3.10 EOL Oct 2026 in Risk Assessment
Tests pass
